name: Bobby Hutcherson
description: American jazz vibraphone and marimba player (1941–2016)
abstract: Robert Hutcherson (January 27, 1941 – August 15, 2016) was an American jazz vibraphone and marimba player. "Little B's Poem", from the 1966 Blue Note album Components, is one of his best-known compositions.

interviews.title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: One Night Stand: A Keyboard Concert
Blue Note Records and WBGO Benefit Jazz Concert
JVC Jazz Festival: Sweet Lorraine: A Jazz Celebration for the Village Vanguard
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: we had a sextet for a small time a couple of years, Dave Burns, Bobby Hutcherson, Herman Wright, Candy Finch and Al and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I remember at some point in the nineties I did a record with Donald Byrd called “A City Called Heaven,” and Bobby Hutcherson, Joe Henderson, Rufus Reid was on it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I first met Bobby Hutcherson when Terry Trotter... took me over to Bobby’s house to play with Bobby and him and Herbie Lewis... I played with them a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: I also produced uh, two for Bobby Hutcherson... So I sort of volunteered... we got together and came out with some pretty good products.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: And I played this festival and this band was Andrew Hill, Bobby Hutcherson, Ron Carter, Roy Hanyes, Joe Henderson and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Bobby Hutcherson, mostly in the seventies, and we’d go to Europe a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002
